name: AWS WAF JSON Body Size Restriction for API Endpoints
description: Restricts oversized JSON request bodies on API paths (for example /api/*) to reduce parser abuse, resource exhaustion, and payload evasion attempts. Designed for phased rollout: count mode first, then block after tuning.
objective: Detect and control oversized JSON bodies sent to API endpoints where large payloads are unnecessary or suspicious.
risk_mitigated: resource exhaustion via large request bodies
WAF inspection evasion using oversized payloads
tuning_notes: Default threshold is 16384 bytes (16 KB). Set per API profile: small CRUD endpoints often fit within 4-16 KB, while bulk upload/import endpoints may require higher limits.
If specific routes need larger JSON bodies, create explicit allow/exception rules (higher priority) for those exact paths before this rule.
Keep Content-Type condition to reduce noise from non-JSON requests; extend with additional JSON media types if used (for example application/merge-patch+json).
invalid_fallback_behavior is set to MATCH to treat malformed/unparsable JSON body handling defensively during rollout.
rollout_guidance: Deploy in COUNT mode first and observe CloudWatch metrics plus sampled requests for at least 7 days.
Review top matched endpoints, client identities, and payload sizes to identify legitimate large-body traffic.
Tune thresholds and add scoped exceptions for known valid large-payload endpoints.
Switch action from COUNT to BLOCK only after false positives are understood and acceptable.
After enforcement, keep monitoring match rates and periodically revalidate endpoint size assumptions.
monitoring_guidance: Track metric apiJsonBodySizeOverLimit for spikes by environment and deployment wave.
Correlate matches with upstream 4xx/5xx API trends and backend saturation indicators.
Alert on sudden increase from a single source IP, ASN, or user-agent cluster.
